reasoning:
The resolution depends on Viswanathan Anand's FIDE ranking according to the Wikipedia page 'FIDE rankings' on January 25 and February 17, 2026. As of January 18 (the forecast date), Anand's rank is exactly 13 based on the January 2026 FIDE rating list. 
Since FIDE updates its ratings and rankings on the 1st of every month, Wikipedia's table for January 2026 is already determined and will remain unchanged until the February list is published. Therefore, on January 25, Anand's ranking will still be exactly 13. A rank of 13 qualifies as 'as high or higher than' 13, making this outcome nearly certain (~98%, accounting for external anomalies/vandalism).
For the February 17 resolution date, Anand's ranking will depend on the February 2026 FIDE rating list. Anand is generally inactive in classical chess, so his 2743 rating is highly likely to remain stable. For his rank to worsen (drop to 14 or lower), a player directly beneath him (such as Richard Rapport at 2741, Leinier Dominguez at 2738, or Maxime Vachier-Lagrave at 2734) would need to play in January events (like the Tata Steel Masters) and gain enough points to overtake him. Conversely, multiple players right above Anand (e.g., Abdusattorov at 2751 or So at 2753) could drop points, which would push Anand up to 12th or higher. Given the mix of participants, fewer immediate chasers are active in January compared to the number of top players who could realistically drop points, keeping his ranking most probabilistically at 13 or better. We estimate this at 85%.
